data.title: Medtronic: Portfolio Streamlining and Diabetes Spin-Off Benefits Offset by Lower Growth Outlook and Valuation Compression, Justifying Hold Rating
data.description:
Analyst Matthew Taylor of Jefferies reiterated a Hold rating on Medtronic, with a price target of $95.00.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Matthew Taylor has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors tied to Medtronic’s updated outlook and valuation. He modestly reduced earnings forecasts for FY26–FY28 to reflect timing of the MMED IPO, one-time MiniMed Flex launch costs, and rising competitive pressure in structural heart and cardiac ablation markets, which temper the near‑term growth profile.

At the same time, Taylor acknowledges that the diabetes spin-off should streamline the portfolio, enhance margin optics, and eventually support EPS accretion via share count reduction. However, with sector multiples compressing and Medtronic’s forward growth expectations lower than previously anticipated, he now applies a reduced earnings multiple, resulting in a $95 price target that leaves limited upside versus the current share price, supporting a Hold stance rather than a more constructive rating.

In another report released yesterday, UBS also assigned a Hold rating to the stock with a $90.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Balanced Risk/Reward Keeps Pool at Hold Amid Margin Pressures, Inventory Build, and Macro Uncertainty
data.description:
William Blair analyst Ryan Merkel has maintained their neutral stance on POOL stock, giving a Hold rating on April 8.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Ryan Merkel has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors that leave the risk/reward profile fairly balanced at current levels. The company exceeded first-quarter EPS expectations on the back of stronger equipment sales, healthier early orders from customers, and some recovery in discretionary pool products, which together suggest that underlying pool demand is stable rather than deteriorating.

At the same time, Pool’s gross margin was pressured by early-buy dynamics and did not benefit as much as anticipated from pricing versus costs, while a larger inventory build ahead of OEM price hikes raises execution risk even if it may aid margins in 2026.
Macro uncertainty, including geopolitical risks and higher interest rates, continues to weigh on visibility for new pool construction, and at roughly 15x next-twelve-month EBITDA, the valuation offers limited downside protection until rates fall and revenue growth with healthier margins becomes more evident.

In another report released on April 8, Wells Fargo also maintained a Hold rating on the stock with a $215.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Balanced Risk-Reward Keeps Crown Castle at Hold Despite Modest Fundamental Upside
data.description:
Matthew Niknam, an analyst from Truist Financial, maintained the Hold rating on Crown Castle. The associated price target was raised to $94.00.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Matthew Niknam has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors related to both upside potential and existing limitations. He sees Crown Castle’s near‑term financial outlook as slightly better than management’s guidance, supported by expectations for modest AFFO per share outperformance and gradual margin gains from ground lease buyouts and operating efficiencies.

At the same time, he notes that much of this improvement is already reflected in consensus estimates and valuation, with U.S. carrier spending in a mature phase of the 5G cycle and only modest re‑acceleration anticipated. While the planned Fiber and Small Cell divestiture will leave Crown Castle as a focused U.S. tower operator with solid pro forma cash flow, the risk‑reward profile at his updated $94 price target appears balanced, justifying a Hold rather than a more aggressive rating.

According to TipRanks, Niknam is a 4-star analyst with an average return of 4.3% and a 59.05% success rate. Niknam covers the Technology sector, focusing on stocks such as Cisco Systems, Corning, and Motorola Solutions.

In another report released today, Wells Fargo also maintained a Hold rating on the stock with a $85.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
